text.title: Retroactive ADR Workflow Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply Retroactive ADR Workflow end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: collect-discovery-signals
step.input: PR comments + chat exports + onboarding-blocker log
step.action: Run `scripts/discover-undocumented-decisions.py` over the last 30 days; produce a digest with signal_kind, occurrences, candidate_owner, priority_score.

step.name: pick-top-3-slices
step.action: Pick top-3 candidates by priority_score; assign a single named owner per candidate; reject any candidate without ≥1 source artefact.

step.name: draft-retro-adr
step.action: For each candidate the owner drafts a Retro-ADR with required frontmatter (id, title, status=accepted-retroactively, decided_on_estimated, authored_on, owner, sources) and the four body sections: Original intent, Current reconstruction, Consequences, Alternatives considered now.

step.name: review-and-merge
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-retro-adr-workflow.py --file <adr>.json`. The owner answers the self-check checklist; reviewers verify r1 (no blame), r3 (intent vs reconstruction split), r5 (single owner).

step.name: retire-or-supersede-legacy
step.action: If the retro-ADR supersedes a prior decision, link it via `supersedes:` and update the prior ADR status to `superseded-retroactively`.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
